articles.headline: Militant Attack on Army Truck in Rajouri District Raises Security Concerns
articles.explainer:
• An army truck was attacked by militants in Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir, leading to heightened security operations.
• The incident underscores the persistent internal security challenges and cross-border threats in the region.
• Security forces have launched a massive search and cordon operation to neutralize the perpetrators.
• This attack prompts a review of existing security protocols and intelligence gathering mechanisms in vulnerable areas.
gsSubTopics: Internal security challenges
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security forces and their mandate in border areas
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
prelimsPoints: Rajouri district is located in the Jammu division of Jammu and Kashmir.
The region is close to the Line of Control (LoC) with Pakistan-occupied Kashmir.
Indian Army's role in counter-terrorism operations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the resurgence of militancy in certain pockets of Jammu and Kashmir.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces in combating cross-border infiltration and local radicalization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government strategies in ensuring peace and stability in the Union Territory.
Suggest comprehensive measures to address both internal and external dimensions of militancy in J&K.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Army personnel conducting search operations in a forested area of Rajouri.
articles.trivia: Rajouri district is historically known for its strategic location and has witnessed significant events during various conflicts.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific, Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded today, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions covered shared security concerns, economic resilience, supply chain diversification, and climate change initiatives.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• Key outcomes included pledges for increased investment, technology transfer, and collaboration on critical and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the QUAD grouping.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the High-Speed Rail.
The dialogue aims to strengthen the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership in maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the potential for enhanced economic cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in critical sectors like semiconductors and renewable energy.
Analyze how the India-Japan relationship contributes to India's broader foreign policy objectives and regional security architecture.
What are the challenges and opportunities in deepening the India-Japan collaboration in a rapidly changing geopolitical landscape?
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan was one of the first countries to recognize India's independence in 1947.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Submits Report on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its detailed report on the implementation aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
• The report recommends strengthening the Data Protection Board's autonomy and enhancing citizen awareness mechanisms.
• It also suggests clearer guidelines for data fiduciary obligations and cross-border data transfers.
• The recommendations aim to ensure robust data privacy while fostering digital economy growth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and certain offshore processing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its implications for individual privacy and businesses.
Analyze the role and independence of the Data Protection Board as envisioned by the Act and the parliamentary committee's recommendations.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing data privacy with national security and economic development in the digital age.
How can India ensure effective implementation of its data protection framework while fostering innovation and cross-border data flows?
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a legal document with digital symbols in the background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.

articles.headline: ISRO Begins Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has initiated preliminary work for the ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• This mission is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, the new mission will involve advanced robotic technologies.
• The project signifies India's growing capabilities in complex interplanetary missions and space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 was India's first lunar probe, launched in 2008.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the lunar South Pole in August 2023.
A sample return mission involves collecting geological samples from a celestial body and returning them to Earth for analysis.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of lunar sample return missions for scientific research and future space exploration endeavors.
Analyze how ISRO's Chandrayaan program has elevated India's standing in global space technology.
Examine the technological challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how India is preparing to overcome them.
What are the socio-economic benefits and potential spin-off technologies from India's advanced space missions?
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a robotic lander collecting samples on the lunar surface.
articles.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was by the Soviet Union's Luna 16 in 1970.

articles.headline: RBI to Expand Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot Program to Include More Banks and Use Cases
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced plans to significantly expand the pilot program for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), or Digital Rupee (e₹).
• The expansion will include more public and private sector banks, as well as new use cases for both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
• The move aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, security, and public acceptance of e₹.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to modernize its payment systems and enhance financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CBDC is a legal tender issued by a central bank in a digital form.
It is different from cryptocurrencies (like Bitcoin) as it is centrally regulated and backed by the central bank.
The RBI launched the wholesale pilot in November 2022 and the retail pilot in December 2022.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India.
Discuss how the Digital Rupee can impact financial inclusion, monetary policy transmission, and the overall payment landscape.
Examine the technological and regulatory challenges faced by the RBI in expanding the CBDC pilot program.
Compare and contrast India's approach to CBDC with other major economies, highlighting unique features and implications.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of digital currency flowing into a mobile wallet.
articles.trivia: The Bahamas was the first country to launch a full-fledged CBDC, the 'Sand Dollar', in 2020.

articles.headline: Delhi Government Announces New Measures to Combat Persistent Air Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The Delhi government unveiled a comprehensive set of new measures aimed at tackling the persistent problem of air pollution in the capital.
• These measures include stricter enforcement of emission norms, promotion of electric vehicles, and enhanced monitoring of industrial and construction activities.
• Special focus will be given to waste management and curbing stubble burning in collaboration with neighboring states.
• The initiative seeks to improve air quality significantly, especially during the winter months.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Delhi is one of the most polluted cities globally, particularly during winter.
Key sources of pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial emissions, dust from construction, and stubble burning.
The Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) is an emergency measure implemented in Delhi-NCR to combat severe air pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Delhi and the National Capital Region.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and interventions to curb air pollution.
Discuss the challenges in implementing long-term solutions, particularly those requiring inter-state cooperation.
Suggest sustainable and technologically advanced strategies for improving air quality in urban centers like Delhi.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred cityscape of Delhi with smog hanging in the air.
articles.trivia: The first documented 'Great Smog' event occurred in London in December 1952, leading to thousands of deaths and prompting modern environmental legislation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Review Petitions on Electoral Bonds Scheme Verdict
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would hear a batch of review petitions challenging its earlier verdict that struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The court had previously declared the scheme unconstitutional, citing violations of the right to information and voter privacy.
• The review petitions seek clarification on certain aspects and reconsideration of the judgment's implications.
• This development comes amidst ongoing debates about transparency in political funding and electoral reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bonds Scheme was introduced in 2017 through the Finance Act, 2017.
It allowed anonymous donations to political parties.
The Supreme Court struck down the scheme in February 2024, citing its unconstitutionality.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's original verdict on the Electoral Bonds Scheme, particularly concerning fundamental rights.
Analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in the context of transparency in political funding.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding democratic principles and ensuring electoral integrity in India.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance donor privacy with public accountability.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: A review petition in the Supreme Court is filed under Article 137 of the Constitution and Order XLVII of the Supreme Court Rules, 2013.

articles.headline: India Mourns Loss of Noted Personalities Across Various Fields
articles.explainer:
• Anil R. Joshi, a revered Gujarati poet and essayist, passed away at 84, leaving behind a rich literary legacy.
• Uttam Mohanty, the veteran Odia actor, died at 66, remembered for his significant contributions to Odia cinema.
• Former Mumbai cricketer Padmakar Shivalkar, 84, known for his left-arm spin, also passed away, marking the end of an era in domestic cricket.
• P. Raju, a 73-year-old politician, and Sunil Satpal Sangwan, 77, another politician, also left a void in public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important personalities
prelimsPoints: Anil R. Joshi was a recipient of the Sahitya Akademi Award.
Uttam Mohanty was a prominent figure in the Odia film industry.
Padmakar Shivalkar played for Mumbai in the Ranji Trophy and was a prolific wicket-taker.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of portraits of the mentioned personalities.
articles.trivia: Padmakar Shivalkar, despite his stellar domestic record, never played for the Indian national cricket team due to the presence of other world-class spinners like Bishan Singh Bedi.

articles.headline: Distinguished Architect and Urologist Pass Away
articles.explainer:
• Bruno Dias Souza, a renowned architect, passed away at the age of 99, celebrated for his significant contributions to modern Indian architecture.
• George P. Abraham, a distinguished urologist and nephrologist, died at 74, leaving a legacy of medical innovation and patient care.
• Their passing marks a loss for their respective fields, highlighting their lasting impact on infrastructure and healthcare in India.
• Both individuals were pioneers who shaped their professions through dedication and expertise.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important personalities
prelimsPoints: Bruno Dias Souza was known for integrating modernist principles with local Indian aesthetics.
George P. Abraham was a leading figure in advanced urological and nephrological treatments.
articles.imageDescription: A sketch of a modern architectural building and a medical symbol.
articles.trivia: Modern Indian architecture, post-independence, saw a blend of international styles with local materials and cultural considerations.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Maritime Security Dialogue in Colombo
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka convened their annual high-level maritime security dialogue in Colombo to enhance bilateral cooperation.
• Discussions focused on combating illegal fishing, maritime terrorism, drug trafficking, and ensuring safety of navigation.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening intelligence sharing and joint naval exercises.
• The dialogue is crucial for maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean region and addressing common security challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Sri Lanka is India's close maritime neighbor in the Indian Ocean.
Fishermen issues in the Palk Strait are a recurring point of discussion.
India's 'SAGAR' vision emphasizes maritime cooperation with littoral states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka maritime cooperation for regional security in the Indian Ocean.
Discuss the challenges in resolving long-standing issues like illegal fishing and their impact on bilateral relations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current mechanisms for maritime security cooperation between the two countries.
Suggest measures to further enhance joint efforts in combating non-traditional security threats in the maritime domain.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise at sea.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait, separating India and Sri Lanka, is named after Robert Palk, a governor of Madras during the British Raj.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Focus areas included curriculum reforms, teacher training, digital education initiatives, and skill development programs.
• States and Union Territories presented their action plans and shared best practices for NEP's phased rollout.
• The review aims to accelerate the transformative changes envisioned by the policy across all levels of education.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: The NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary education, vocational training, and digital literacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy 2020 and their potential to transform India's education landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a policy of such scale and scope, particularly concerning resource allocation and federal cooperation.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital initiatives in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020.
How can stakeholder engagement and continuous evaluation ensure the successful and equitable implementation of the policy across diverse regions?
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Annual Meeting of Permanent Indus Commission
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held their annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission as mandated by the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The discussions focused on various technical aspects of the treaty, including data exchange and planned hydropower projects on both sides.
• The meeting aims to resolve any potential disputes amicably and ensure the smooth implementation of the water-sharing agreement.
• Despite political tensions, the Indus Waters Treaty remains one of the most enduring water-sharing agreements globally.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers to India and the Western Rivers to Pakistan.
The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meets annually to discuss issues related to the treaty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for transboundary water sharing, despite persistent political tensions.
Discuss the challenges faced by both India and Pakistan in adhering to the treaty provisions, particularly concerning hydropower projects.
Evaluate the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving disputes and ensuring the continued functionality of the agreement.
What are the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity for the long-term viability of the Indus Waters Treaty?
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands, Focus on Interoperability
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced its expansion to integrate more healthcare providers and digital health solutions.
• A key focus is on achieving seamless interoperability between various digital health systems and applications.
• This expansion aims to create a comprehensive digital health ecosystem, enhancing access and efficiency of healthcare services.
• The mission seeks to provide every Indian citizen with a unique health ID and digitized health records.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in 2021 by the National Health Authority (NHA).
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare, connecting patients, doctors, and other stakeholders.
The mission is built on the principles of consent-based data sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the challenges involved in ensuring data privacy, security, and widespread adoption of digital health solutions across diverse populations.
Evaluate how interoperability within the digital health ecosystem can improve patient outcomes and resource utilization.
Suggest measures to overcome digital divide and ensure equitable access to digital health services for all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital health services.
articles.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the apex body responsible for implementing Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) and the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Widening Global Climate Finance Gap for Developing Nations
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations highlighted a significant and widening gap in climate finance for developing countries.
• The report indicates that current financial flows fall far short of what is needed to meet climate adaptation and mitigation targets.
• It calls for developed nations to honor their commitments and scale up financial support, including grants and concessional loans.
• The findings will be crucial for upcoming international climate negotiations, emphasizing the need for innovative financing mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Climate change
International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Developed nations committed to mobilizing $100 billion annually for climate finance to developing countries by 2020.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC to assist developing countries.
Adaptation refers to adjusting to actual or expected future climate; Mitigation refers to reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of 'climate finance gap' and its implications for developing countries' ability to combat climate change.
Analyze the responsibilities of developed nations in providing climate finance under the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR).
Examine the challenges in mobilizing and effectively utilizing climate finance, including issues of access and transparency.
Suggest innovative financing mechanisms and policy reforms that can bridge the climate finance gap and accelerate global climate action.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing a widening gap between climate finance needs and actual flows.
articles.trivia: The 'Loss and Damage' fund, aimed at assisting vulnerable nations cope with climate impacts, was operationalized at COP28 in Dubai.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Continues Robust Growth, Targets Achievable
articles.explainer:
• A recent government report highlighted the continued robust growth in India's renewable energy capacity, primarily solar and wind power.
• The country is well on track to achieve its ambitious targets for renewable energy installation by 2030.
• Significant investments are being made in green hydrogen production and energy storage solutions.
• This growth is crucial for India's energy security, climate commitments, and economic development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Solar energy constitutes the largest share of India's renewable energy mix.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's progress and challenges in transitioning to a renewable energy-dominated power sector.
Analyze the role of government policies and initiatives in accelerating renewable energy growth and achieving climate targets.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen as a future energy source and India's strategy for its development.
What are the implications of increased renewable energy penetration for India's energy security and economic resilience?
articles.imageDescription: A landscape with solar panels and wind turbines under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: The world's largest solar park, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in the Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region and the need for continued conservation efforts.
• The new species, a type of frog, has unique characteristics that distinguish it from known species.
• This finding underscores the importance of ecological research and protecting critical habitats from degradation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
It is home to a large number of endemic species, particularly amphibians and reptiles.
Amphibians are bio-indicators of environmental health.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered frog species.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: Government Boosts 'Start-up India' Initiative with New Funding and Mentorship Programs
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government unveiled new measures to further bolster the 'Start-up India' initiative, focusing on emerging sectors.
• These include increased funding allocations for seed capital and venture debt, along with expanded mentorship programs.
• The aim is to foster a more vibrant innovation ecosystem and encourage entrepreneurship across diverse regions.
• Special emphasis is placed on supporting startups in deep tech, rural development, and social impact sectors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
articles.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: The 'Start-up India' initiative was launched in 2016.
DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) is the nodal agency for the initiative.
India ranks among the top countries globally in terms of the number of startups and unicorns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the 'Start-up India' initiative in fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem and driving economic growth in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by startups in India, particularly concerning access to finance, regulatory hurdles, and market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government support mechanisms, such as funding and mentorship, in promoting innovation.
Suggest further policy interventions to empower startups in emerging sectors and ensure inclusive growth across different regions of India.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
articles.trivia: India has the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tribal Affairs Launches New Scheme for Promotion of Tribal Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs launched a new scheme aimed at preserving and promoting the rich cultural heritage of India's tribal communities.
• The scheme includes provisions for documenting indigenous art forms, languages, and traditional knowledge systems.
• It also focuses on setting up tribal museums, cultural centers, and supporting tribal artisans and performers.
• This initiative seeks to empower tribal communities and ensure the intergenerational transfer of their unique cultural identity.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Effects of globalization on Indian society
prelimsPoints: India has a significant tribal population, recognized under the Fifth and Sixth Schedules of the Constitution.
The Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for overall policy, planning, and coordination of programs for tribal development.
Many tribal languages are endangered and require special efforts for preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving tribal cultural heritage for India's diverse social fabric and national identity.
Analyze the challenges faced by tribal communities in maintaining their cultural identity in the face of modernization and globalization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes and policies aimed at promoting tribal art, language, and traditional knowledge.
Suggest inclusive strategies to empower tribal communities as custodians of their culture while ensuring their socio-economic development.
articles.imageDescription: Tribal artists performing a traditional dance in colorful attire.
articles.trivia: The 'Pesa Act' (Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas Act) of 1996 empowers tribal communities with self-governance over their natural resources.

highlights: Militant attack on army truck in Rajouri, Jammu and Kashmir, raises significant security concerns.
India and Japan hold strategic dialogue, boosting cooperation in Indo-Pacific and economic resilience.
Parliamentary panel submits report on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, emphasizing autonomy of Data Protection Board.
ISRO initiates preparations for Chandrayaan-4, an ambitious lunar sample return mission.
RBI announces expansion of Digital Rupee (e₹) pilot program to include more banks and use cases.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, consider the following statements:
1. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
2. It applies to the processing of personal data outside India only if it is for profiling of Data Principals within India.
3. The Act replaced the Information Technology Act, 2000.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India. Statement 2 is incorrect; it applies to processing of digital personal data outside India if it is in connection with offering goods or services to Data Principals in India, or profiling such Data Principals. Statement 3 is incorrect; it replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, not the entire IT Act, 2000, though it amends certain provisions of the IT Act.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Indus Waters Treaty is/are correct?
1. It was brokered by the United Nations.
2. It allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
3. The Permanent Indus Commission meets annually to discuss treaty-related issues.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the treaty was brokered by the World Bank. Statement 2 is correct; India has unrestricted use of the Eastern Rivers. Statement 3 is correct; the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is mandated to meet annually.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about India's renewable energy targets:
1. India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
2. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
3. Solar energy currently constitutes the largest share of India's installed renewable energy capacity.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India has indeed set an ambitious target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030. The Green Hydrogen Mission is a flagship initiative. Solar energy dominates India's renewable energy mix.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'e₹-R' sometimes seen in the news, refers to:
options: (b) Retail segment of India's Central Bank Digital Currency
(c) Reserve Bank's new Electronic Remittance system
(d) Exchange Rate for Rupee in international markets
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Retail segment of India's Central Bank Digital Currency
prelimsMocks.explanation: e₹-R stands for the retail segment of India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), also known as the Digital Rupee. e₹-W refers to the wholesale segment.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following personalities passed away on February 26, 2025, and was a distinguished figure in Gujarati literature?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Anil R. Joshi, 84, was a prominent Gujarati poet and essayist. Uttam Mohanty was an Odia actor, P. Raju a politician, and Padmakar Shivalkar a cricketer.

mainsMocks.question: The recent militant attack in Rajouri highlights the persistent internal security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir. Critically analyze the evolving nature of militancy in the region and suggest comprehensive strategies for ensuring long-term peace and stability.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Rajouri incident. Discuss factors contributing to militancy (cross-border, local radicalization, socio-economic factors). Analyze the evolving nature (hybrid terrorists, use of technology, targeting civilians/security forces). Suggest strategies: robust border management, intelligence gathering, community engagement, economic development, political dialogue, counter-radicalization programs, judicial reforms.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' with Japan is pivotal for promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific. Discuss the key dimensions of this partnership and the challenges it faces in the current geopolitical landscape.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Define the Indo-Pacific concept and its significance for India and Japan. Detail key dimensions: security cooperation (QUAD, naval exercises), economic collaboration (investment, infrastructure, supply chains), technology partnership (5G, AI, semiconductors), multilateral cooperation. Discuss challenges: China's assertiveness, regional rivalries, economic protectionism, internal domestic priorities.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step towards safeguarding citizen privacy while fostering digital growth. Examine the core provisions of the Act and critically evaluate the recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee for its effective implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies. Right to Privacy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Act's purpose. Explain core provisions: definitions (Data Principal, Fiduciary), consent framework, obligations of data fiduciaries, rights of data principals, Data Protection Board, penalties, cross-border data transfer. Critically evaluate committee recommendations: autonomy of DPB, awareness, clear guidelines, balancing innovation and privacy. Discuss challenges: enforcement, digital literacy, potential for misuse, international harmonization.

quickBytes.text: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education.
The Western Ghats are one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots, recognized for their rich endemic species.
The PESA Act (Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas Act) of 1996 empowers tribal communities with self-governance.
India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030, a key climate commitment.
The Digital Rupee (e₹) is a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) issued by the Reserve Bank of India.
The Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) is an emergency measure to combat severe air pollution in Delhi-NCR.
Article 137 of the Indian Constitution grants the Supreme Court the power to review its own judgments.
The first successful lunar sample return mission was by the Soviet Union's Luna 16 in 1970.
India has the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.
The 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision emphasizes India's maritime cooperation with littoral states.

GS1.headline: India Mourns Loss of Noted Personalities Across Various Fields
GS1.explainer:
• Anil R. Joshi, a revered Gujarati poet and essayist, passed away at 84, leaving behind a rich literary legacy.
• Uttam Mohanty, the veteran Odia actor, died at 66, remembered for his significant contributions to Odia cinema.
• Former Mumbai cricketer Padmakar Shivalkar, 84, known for his left-arm spin, also passed away, marking the end of an era in domestic cricket.
• P. Raju, a 73-year-old politician, and Sunil Satpal Sangwan, 77, another politician, also left a void in public service.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important personalities
prelimsPoints: Anil R. Joshi was a recipient of the Sahitya Akademi Award.
Uttam Mohanty was a prominent figure in the Odia film industry.
Padmakar Shivalkar played for Mumbai in the Ranji Trophy and was a prolific wicket-taker.
GS1.imageDescription: A collage of portraits of the mentioned personalities.
GS1.trivia: Padmakar Shivalkar, despite his stellar domestic record, never played for the Indian national cricket team due to the presence of other world-class spinners like Bishan Singh Bedi.

GS1.headline: Distinguished Architect and Urologist Pass Away
GS1.explainer:
• Bruno Dias Souza, a renowned architect, passed away at the age of 99, celebrated for his significant contributions to modern Indian architecture.
• George P. Abraham, a distinguished urologist and nephrologist, died at 74, leaving a legacy of medical innovation and patient care.
• Their passing marks a loss for their respective fields, highlighting their lasting impact on infrastructure and healthcare in India.
• Both individuals were pioneers who shaped their professions through dedication and expertise.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important personalities
prelimsPoints: Bruno Dias Souza was known for integrating modernist principles with local Indian aesthetics.
George P. Abraham was a leading figure in advanced urological and nephrological treatments.
GS1.imageDescription: A sketch of a modern architectural building and a medical symbol.
GS1.trivia: Modern Indian architecture, post-independence, saw a blend of international styles with local materials and cultural considerations.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tribal Affairs Launches New Scheme for Promotion of Tribal Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs launched a new scheme aimed at preserving and promoting the rich cultural heritage of India's tribal communities.
• The scheme includes provisions for documenting indigenous art forms, languages, and traditional knowledge systems.
• It also focuses on setting up tribal museums, cultural centers, and supporting tribal artisans and performers.
• This initiative seeks to empower tribal communities and ensure the intergenerational transfer of their unique cultural identity.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Effects of globalization on Indian society
prelimsPoints: India has a significant tribal population, recognized under the Fifth and Sixth Schedules of the Constitution.
The Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for overall policy, planning, and coordination of programs for tribal development.
Many tribal languages are endangered and require special efforts for preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving tribal cultural heritage for India's diverse social fabric and national identity.
Analyze the challenges faced by tribal communities in maintaining their cultural identity in the face of modernization and globalization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes and policies aimed at promoting tribal art, language, and traditional knowledge.
Suggest inclusive strategies to empower tribal communities as custodians of their culture while ensuring their socio-economic development.
GS1.imageDescription: Tribal artists performing a traditional dance in colorful attire.
GS1.trivia: The 'Pesa Act' (Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas Act) of 1996 empowers tribal communities with self-governance over their natural resources.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific, Economic Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded today, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions covered shared security concerns, economic resilience, supply chain diversification, and climate change initiatives.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• Key outcomes included pledges for increased investment, technology transfer, and collaboration on critical and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the QUAD grouping.
Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the High-Speed Rail.
The dialogue aims to strengthen the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership in maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the potential for enhanced economic cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in critical sectors like semiconductors and renewable energy.
Analyze how the India-Japan relationship contributes to India's broader foreign policy objectives and regional security architecture.
What are the challenges and opportunities in deepening the India-Japan collaboration in a rapidly changing geopolitical landscape?
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan was one of the first countries to recognize India's independence in 1947.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Submits Report on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its detailed report on the implementation aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
• The report recommends strengthening the Data Protection Board's autonomy and enhancing citizen awareness mechanisms.
• It also suggests clearer guidelines for data fiduciary obligations and cross-border data transfers.
• The recommendations aim to ensure robust data privacy while fostering digital economy growth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and certain offshore processing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its implications for individual privacy and businesses.
Analyze the role and independence of the Data Protection Board as envisioned by the Act and the parliamentary committee's recommendations.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing data privacy with national security and economic development in the digital age.
How can India ensure effective implementation of its data protection framework while fostering innovation and cross-border data flows?
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a legal document with digital symbols in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Review Petitions on Electoral Bonds Scheme Verdict
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would hear a batch of review petitions challenging its earlier verdict that struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The court had previously declared the scheme unconstitutional, citing violations of the right to information and voter privacy.
• The review petitions seek clarification on certain aspects and reconsideration of the judgment's implications.
• This development comes amidst ongoing debates about transparency in political funding and electoral reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bonds Scheme was introduced in 2017 through the Finance Act, 2017.
It allowed anonymous donations to political parties.
The Supreme Court struck down the scheme in February 2024, citing its unconstitutionality.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's original verdict on the Electoral Bonds Scheme, particularly concerning fundamental rights.
Analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in the context of transparency in political funding.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding democratic principles and ensuring electoral integrity in India.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance donor privacy with public accountability.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: A review petition in the Supreme Court is filed under Article 137 of the Constitution and Order XLVII of the Supreme Court Rules, 2013.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Maritime Security Dialogue in Colombo
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka convened their annual high-level maritime security dialogue in Colombo to enhance bilateral cooperation.
• Discussions focused on combating illegal fishing, maritime terrorism, drug trafficking, and ensuring safety of navigation.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening intelligence sharing and joint naval exercises.
• The dialogue is crucial for maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean region and addressing common security challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Sri Lanka is India's close maritime neighbor in the Indian Ocean.
Fishermen issues in the Palk Strait are a recurring point of discussion.
India's 'SAGAR' vision emphasizes maritime cooperation with littoral states.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka maritime cooperation for regional security in the Indian Ocean.
Discuss the challenges in resolving long-standing issues like illegal fishing and their impact on bilateral relations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current mechanisms for maritime security cooperation between the two countries.
Suggest measures to further enhance joint efforts in combating non-traditional security threats in the maritime domain.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise at sea.
GS2.trivia: The Palk Strait, separating India and Sri Lanka, is named after Robert Palk, a governor of Madras during the British Raj.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Focus areas included curriculum reforms, teacher training, digital education initiatives, and skill development programs.
• States and Union Territories presented their action plans and shared best practices for NEP's phased rollout.
• The review aims to accelerate the transformative changes envisioned by the policy across all levels of education.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: The NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary education, vocational training, and digital literacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy 2020 and their potential to transform India's education landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a policy of such scale and scope, particularly concerning resource allocation and federal cooperation.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital initiatives in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020.
How can stakeholder engagement and continuous evaluation ensure the successful and equitable implementation of the policy across diverse regions?
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Annual Meeting of Permanent Indus Commission
GS2.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held their annual meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission as mandated by the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The discussions focused on various technical aspects of the treaty, including data exchange and planned hydropower projects on both sides.
• The meeting aims to resolve any potential disputes amicably and ensure the smooth implementation of the water-sharing agreement.
• Despite political tensions, the Indus Waters Treaty remains one of the most enduring water-sharing agreements globally.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers to India and the Western Rivers to Pakistan.
The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meets annually to discuss issues related to the treaty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for transboundary water sharing, despite persistent political tensions.
Discuss the challenges faced by both India and Pakistan in adhering to the treaty provisions, particularly concerning hydropower projects.
Evaluate the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in resolving disputes and ensuring the continued functionality of the agreement.
What are the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity for the long-term viability of the Indus Waters Treaty?
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands, Focus on Interoperability
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced its expansion to integrate more healthcare providers and digital health solutions.
• A key focus is on achieving seamless interoperability between various digital health systems and applications.
• This expansion aims to create a comprehensive digital health ecosystem, enhancing access and efficiency of healthcare services.
• The mission seeks to provide every Indian citizen with a unique health ID and digitized health records.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in 2021 by the National Health Authority (NHA).
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare, connecting patients, doctors, and other stakeholders.
The mission is built on the principles of consent-based data sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the challenges involved in ensuring data privacy, security, and widespread adoption of digital health solutions across diverse populations.
Evaluate how interoperability within the digital health ecosystem can improve patient outcomes and resource utilization.
Suggest measures to overcome digital divide and ensure equitable access to digital health services for all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital health services.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the apex body responsible for implementing Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) and the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).

GS3.headline: Militant Attack on Army Truck in Rajouri District Raises Security Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• An army truck was attacked by militants in Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir, leading to heightened security operations.
• The incident underscores the persistent internal security challenges and cross-border threats in the region.
• Security forces have launched a massive search and cordon operation to neutralize the perpetrators.
• This attack prompts a review of existing security protocols and intelligence gathering mechanisms in vulnerable areas.
gsSubTopics: Internal security challenges
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security forces and their mandate in border areas
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
prelimsPoints: Rajouri district is located in the Jammu division of Jammu and Kashmir.
The region is close to the Line of Control (LoC) with Pakistan-occupied Kashmir.
Indian Army's role in counter-terrorism operations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the resurgence of militancy in certain pockets of Jammu and Kashmir.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces in combating cross-border infiltration and local radicalization.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government strategies in ensuring peace and stability in the Union Territory.
Suggest comprehensive measures to address both internal and external dimensions of militancy in J&K.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Army personnel conducting search operations in a forested area of Rajouri.
GS3.trivia: Rajouri district is historically known for its strategic location and has witnessed significant events during various conflicts.

GS3.headline: ISRO Begins Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has initiated preliminary work for the ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• This mission is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission, aiming to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, the new mission will involve advanced robotic technologies.
• The project signifies India's growing capabilities in complex interplanetary missions and space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 was India's first lunar probe, launched in 2008.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the lunar South Pole in August 2023.
A sample return mission involves collecting geological samples from a celestial body and returning them to Earth for analysis.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of lunar sample return missions for scientific research and future space exploration endeavors.
Analyze how ISRO's Chandrayaan program has elevated India's standing in global space technology.
Examine the technological challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how India is preparing to overcome them.
What are the socio-economic benefits and potential spin-off technologies from India's advanced space missions?
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a robotic lander collecting samples on the lunar surface.
GS3.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was by the Soviet Union's Luna 16 in 1970.

GS3.headline: RBI to Expand Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot Program to Include More Banks and Use Cases
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced plans to significantly expand the pilot program for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), or Digital Rupee (e₹).
• The expansion will include more public and private sector banks, as well as new use cases for both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
• The move aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, security, and public acceptance of e₹.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to modernize its payment systems and enhance financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: CBDC is a legal tender issued by a central bank in a digital form.
It is different from cryptocurrencies (like Bitcoin) as it is centrally regulated and backed by the central bank.
The RBI launched the wholesale pilot in November 2022 and the retail pilot in December 2022.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India.
Discuss how the Digital Rupee can impact financial inclusion, monetary policy transmission, and the overall payment landscape.
Examine the technological and regulatory challenges faced by the RBI in expanding the CBDC pilot program.
Compare and contrast India's approach to CBDC with other major economies, highlighting unique features and implications.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of digital currency flowing into a mobile wallet.
GS3.trivia: The Bahamas was the first country to launch a full-fledged CBDC, the 'Sand Dollar', in 2020.

GS3.headline: Delhi Government Announces New Measures to Combat Persistent Air Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The Delhi government unveiled a comprehensive set of new measures aimed at tackling the persistent problem of air pollution in the capital.
• These measures include stricter enforcement of emission norms, promotion of electric vehicles, and enhanced monitoring of industrial and construction activities.
• Special focus will be given to waste management and curbing stubble burning in collaboration with neighboring states.
• The initiative seeks to improve air quality significantly, especially during the winter months.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Delhi is one of the most polluted cities globally, particularly during winter.
Key sources of pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial emissions, dust from construction, and stubble burning.
The Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) is an emergency measure implemented in Delhi-NCR to combat severe air pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Delhi and the National Capital Region.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and interventions to curb air pollution.
Discuss the challenges in implementing long-term solutions, particularly those requiring inter-state cooperation.
Suggest sustainable and technologically advanced strategies for improving air quality in urban centers like Delhi.
GS3.imageDescription: A blurred cityscape of Delhi with smog hanging in the air.
GS3.trivia: The first documented 'Great Smog' event occurred in London in December 1952, leading to thousands of deaths and prompting modern environmental legislation.

GS3.headline: UN Report Highlights Widening Global Climate Finance Gap for Developing Nations
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations highlighted a significant and widening gap in climate finance for developing countries.
• The report indicates that current financial flows fall far short of what is needed to meet climate adaptation and mitigation targets.
• It calls for developed nations to honor their commitments and scale up financial support, including grants and concessional loans.
• The findings will be crucial for upcoming international climate negotiations, emphasizing the need for innovative financing mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Climate change
International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Developed nations committed to mobilizing $100 billion annually for climate finance to developing countries by 2020.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC to assist developing countries.
Adaptation refers to adjusting to actual or expected future climate; Mitigation refers to reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of 'climate finance gap' and its implications for developing countries' ability to combat climate change.
Analyze the responsibilities of developed nations in providing climate finance under the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR).
Examine the challenges in mobilizing and effectively utilizing climate finance, including issues of access and transparency.
Suggest innovative financing mechanisms and policy reforms that can bridge the climate finance gap and accelerate global climate action.
GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing a widening gap between climate finance needs and actual flows.
GS3.trivia: The 'Loss and Damage' fund, aimed at assisting vulnerable nations cope with climate impacts, was operationalized at COP28 in Dubai.

GS3.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Continues Robust Growth, Targets Achievable
GS3.explainer:
• A recent government report highlighted the continued robust growth in India's renewable energy capacity, primarily solar and wind power.
• The country is well on track to achieve its ambitious targets for renewable energy installation by 2030.
• Significant investments are being made in green hydrogen production and energy storage solutions.
• This growth is crucial for India's energy security, climate commitments, and economic development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Solar energy constitutes the largest share of India's renewable energy mix.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's progress and challenges in transitioning to a renewable energy-dominated power sector.
Analyze the role of government policies and initiatives in accelerating renewable energy growth and achieving climate targets.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen as a future energy source and India's strategy for its development.
What are the implications of increased renewable energy penetration for India's energy security and economic resilience?
GS3.imageDescription: A landscape with solar panels and wind turbines under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: The world's largest solar park, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in the Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region and the need for continued conservation efforts.
• The new species, a type of frog, has unique characteristics that distinguish it from known species.
• This finding underscores the importance of ecological research and protecting critical habitats from degradation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
It is home to a large number of endemic species, particularly amphibians and reptiles.
Amphibians are bio-indicators of environmental health.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered frog species.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts 'Start-up India' Initiative with New Funding and Mentorship Programs
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government unveiled new measures to further bolster the 'Start-up India' initiative, focusing on emerging sectors.
• These include increased funding allocations for seed capital and venture debt, along with expanded mentorship programs.
• The aim is to foster a more vibrant innovation ecosystem and encourage entrepreneurship across diverse regions.
• Special emphasis is placed on supporting startups in deep tech, rural development, and social impact sectors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
GS3.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: The 'Start-up India' initiative was launched in 2016.
DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) is the nodal agency for the initiative.
India ranks among the top countries globally in terms of the number of startups and unicorns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the 'Start-up India' initiative in fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem and driving economic growth in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by startups in India, particularly concerning access to finance, regulatory hurdles, and market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government support mechanisms, such as funding and mentorship, in promoting innovation.
Suggest further policy interventions to empower startups in emerging sectors and ensure inclusive growth across different regions of India.
GS3.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
GS3.trivia: India has the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
